subparts.text: Define the term perfect secrecy in cryptography. Provide a suitable example. 2.5
Differentiate between symmetric and asymmetric encryption with appropriate justification. 2.5
Explain the purpose and significance of a one-time pad in cryptographic systems. 2.5
Explain use of the Euler Totient Function , ϕ in RSA 2.5
What is Secured Socket Layer ? Explain its applications 2.5
What is the output length produced when the SHA-1 hash function is applied to a 512-bit input? 2.5
Describe how a digital signature ensures authenticity in secure communication. 2.5
Explain how SSL/TLS protocols contribute to maintaining security over the Internet. 2.5

questions.question_text: Explain the working of classical encryption techniques such as substitution and transposition ciphers. Support your explanation with detailed examples.
Describe the working principle of the Advanced Encryption Standard . Explain its various modes of operation with suitable illustrations.

subparts.text: Calculate the public keys generated by Alice and Bob.
Compute the final shared secret key derived by both parties.
Using this key, compute the ciphertext corresponding to the ASCII value of the character ‘E’ .

questions.question_text: Define stream ciphers in the context of cryptography. Provide one appropriate example illustrating its working.
Compare and contrast Message Authentication Codes and cryptographic hash functions. Discuss their roles in ensuring data integrity and authenticity, supported with relevant examples.
Discuss the role of SSL/TLS protocols in securing online communication. Explain their working mechanism and significance, using a suitable example.
Explain the steps involved in securing email communication using Pretty Good Privacy . Illustrate how confidentiality, authentication, and integrity are ensured.
